reasons: Fredrinn gives Estes a durable jungle/frontline anchor who can stay in the fight long enough to benefit from repeated healing.
Estes can sustain Fredrinn and nearby teammates while Fredrinn absorbs pressure around objectives.

proof.summary: Fredrinn absorbs pressure and holds the frontline, while Estes sustains him and nearby teammates through extended fights.
worksBestWhen: Fredrinn fights within Estes' healing range.
The team groups around objective fights.
failureCases: Estes is picked off before the fight.
Enemy anti-heal and burst are applied early.

reasons: Barats can act as a durable frontline body while Estes extends his fight duration.
Estes helps Barats survive long enough to pressure targets and hold space for the team.

proof.summary: Barats gives Estes a durable frontliner to sustain, while Estes helps Barats survive long enough to pressure or isolate targets.
worksBestWhen: Barats controls the fight near Estes.
The team follows Barats' target focus.

reasons: Alpha likes extended melee skirmishes, and Estes adds extra sustain so Alpha can keep trading through long fights.
Estes can keep Alpha healthy while Alpha applies sustained melee pressure near the frontline.

proof.summary: Alpha brings sustained melee pressure, while Estes strengthens his ability to keep fighting near the team.
worksBestWhen: Alpha fights within Estes' healing area.
The team commits to extended fights instead of scattered dives.
failureCases: Estes is forced away before Alpha finishes the fight.

reasons: Hanabi is a cover-dependent teamfight marksman, so Estes' grouped sustain directly supports her main fight pattern.
Estes gives Hanabi more time to use bouncing basic attacks from behind the frontline.

proof.summary: Estes sustains Hanabi in grouped fights, giving her more time to use bouncing basic attacks and teamfight damage from behind cover.
worksBestWhen: Hanabi stays near Estes and the frontline.
The team plays grouped objective fights.
failureCases: Enemy burst kills Hanabi before healing matters.

reasons: Ixia's Full Barrage creates a large teamfight damage window but requires cover while she fires.
Estes sustains Ixia and the frontline formation that protects her during the stationary damage window.

proof.summary: Ixia creates a stationary damage zone with Full Barrage, while Estes sustains the formation that protects her during that immobile window.
worksBestWhen: Ixia casts Full Barrage behind frontline cover.
Estes and the team group around her firing zone.
failureCases: Ixia is flanked during Full Barrage.
The enemy uses suppression or displacement timing.
